title: Brasileirão standings/team-stats double-count overlapping match files
evidence: soccer/loader.go:155 tags Brasileirao_Matches.csv and soccer/loader.go:343 tags novo_campeonato_brasileiro.csv both as competition="brasileirao" with no de-duplication; seasons 2012–2019 overlap (2019 has 380 rows in EACH file). soccer/store.go:148 GetStandings and store.go:92 GetTeamStats iterate all rows, so a team shows ~76 played / doubled points for a 38-game season. GoalsAverage (store.go:350) is also computed over the duplicated set.
suggestion: De-duplicate matches on (competition, season, date, home, away) at load time, or pick one canonical brasileirão source per season range before aggregating standings, team stats, and averages.

title: ATDD acceptance tests share a global pre-loaded store, not an empty per-scenario system
evidence: acceptance_test.go:14-23 loads one package-global testStore from the real data/kaggle dataset in TestMain; every AT01–AT12 scenario (e.g. acceptance_test.go:45,141) reuses it. The ATDD prompt requires each scenario to 'start from a running but empty system and share no data with other tests'.
suggestion: Build a fresh store seeded with a small, scenario-specific fixture inside each test (or a per-test setup helper) instead of sharing one globally loaded production dataset.

title: Acceptance assertions only check substring presence, not computed values
evidence: acceptance_test.go:147-164 (AT07 standings) only asserts Flamengo appears near the top; acceptance_test.go:98-111 (AT04 team stats) only asserts the words win/draw/loss appear. No test checks exact W/L/D, points, or match counts — so the R9 double-counting defect passes undetected.
suggestion: Assert on concrete computed values against a controlled fixture (e.g. exact points, played count, head-to-head tallies) so aggregation bugs fail a test.

title: Match filtering supports season but not an explicit date range
evidence: soccer/store.go:48 filters by Season only; MatchFilter (store.go:9) has no from/to date fields, and find_matches (tools/tools.go:344) exposes only season. R4 mentions 'date range and/or season'.
suggestion: Add optional date_from/date_to parameters to find_matches and MatchFilter for full date-range support.

title: get_statistics silently defaults unknown stat_type to goals_average
evidence: tools/tools.go:275-277 — an unrecognized stat_type falls through to handleGoalsAverage with no error, masking client typos.
suggestion: Return a clear error listing valid stat_type values (biggest_wins, goals_average, home_away_record) instead of silently substituting goals_average.

title: Stored test coverage is low (13.4%)
evidence: scores.json / retort.db test_coverage=0.134; the large CSV loader paths (soccer/loader.go) and tool formatters are only indirectly exercised. defect_rate=1.0 confirms build+tests pass.
suggestion: Add unit tests for the per-file CSV loaders and formatting helpers to raise coverage and pin parsing behavior.

title: Token efficiency score is very low (0.07)
evidence: scores.json / retort.db token_efficiency=0.0709; maintainability=0.490, idiomatic=0.58 — informational cross-run signals, not defects.
suggestion: No action required for correctness; recorded for cross-run comparison.
